lines.text: Tucker Traywick, East Side perimeter, near Schenk's Corners. We're {{ELAPSED}} in. Madison-wide cumulative is sitting at 139 kills as of 02:18. That's tracking four kills behind last year at this exact point — 02:00 hour into the night, we were at 143.

Neighborhood weight: East Side is still carrying the load. Thirty-four kills here alone, which is 24 percent of the total metro count. That's a seven-point-two percent concentration increase from 2168.
Holden, you seeing the weapons-trade pattern shift we discussed earlier? I'm watching the Atwood Avenue corridor and the bladed-implement traffic is heavier than the early hours suggested.

Yeah, I see it. Firearms percentage is going to drop again this year — I'd estimate we'll finish around forty-one, forty-two percent if the trend holds through dawn. Your field data's matching the aggregate. The economic pressure is real.

Copy. One thing I need to flag — and this might be nothing — but I haven't received a check-in from Darren. You know, Atwood duplex, the PurgeShield panel caller from last year. He texts the station every year around this hour.

It's 02:27 now. Cold's settling in hard. Visibility dropping. I'm repositioning north toward the Lake Mendota approach — the sleet pattern is changing and I want to get ahead of any shoreline activity before the fog closes in completely. Hold on, my signal's —

tickerItems.text: Madison-wide cumulative: 139 ({{ELAPSED}} in). YoY delta: -4 kills. East Side concentration: 24.5%. Pace tracking 181–189 final tally.
East Side (Atwood/Schenk's): 34 kills. Bladed-implement traffic up 7.2% from early hours. Firearms percentage trending down — estimated final: 41–42%.
Darren (Atwood regular): no check-in at 02:27. Checked in at 22:40 last year. Station monitoring.
Lake Mendota visibility declining. Sleet pattern shifting. Fog expected 02:45–03:15. Shoreline risk elevated.
